summary: This is a well-established 15-year-old cleaning service with a clear boomer retirement exit, semi-absentee operations, and a strong payback period under 3 years. The recurring monthly contracts and existing part-time staff make for a clean transition. The primary concern is the stated profit margin of 33% — more than double the industry norm — which warrants careful due diligence on actual expenses, labor costs, and whether the owner is compensating themselves below market rate.
key_strength: True semi-absentee model with 8 hrs/week owner involvement, 3 existing staff, recurring contract revenue, and a motivated retiring seller — the ideal acquisition profile for this thesis.
key_risk: Profit margin of 33.3% is suspiciously high for a cleaning service (industry norm ~14%). The cash flow figure may not account for full labor replacement costs, vehicle maintenance, insurance, or a market-rate management salary. Verify all expenses line-by-line.
negotiation_note: Strong leverage: owner is selling due to age/health (urgency), the margin anomaly raises questions about true profitability, and the 2.67x multiple is already below typical 3-5x for cleaning services. Could push for $30K-$35K by citing margin concerns and offering a quick close. Health-motivated sellers often prioritize speed over price.
